chosen_action.title: Stop publisher-overlap guard pauses from suppressing concrete empty-board repairs after re-entry repairs are exhausted

why_this_action.summary: Codeberg was still flat, the execution board still had no truthful do-now packet, and the live loop had regressed into distribution_architecture_guard_pause even after both hold-window re-entry repairs were already consumed. The highest-leverage move available in this slot was to repair selector behavior so the loop converts that state into a concrete distribution-architecture repair instead of preserving another fake-idle guard pause.
shared_findings_used: distribution_lane_latest.json: the live selector had just chosen distribution_architecture_guard_pause at 2026-05-28T06:38:39 with the board still empty
marketing_execution_board_latest.md: no truthful do-now handoff packet existed in the current review window
marketing_workflow_audit_latest.json: primary_repo_flat and same-family publisher overlap were still in measurement-pending state
market_intelligence_latest.json / adoption_metrics_latest.md: Codeberg movement remained the primary success gate, so empty-board churn had to be treated as a process failure

result.summary: Patched distribution_lane_selector so publisher-overlap hold logic no longer preserves distribution_architecture_guard_pause after both short-window re-entry repairs are already spent, updated the regression test to require distribution_architecture_repair in that state, ran focused unit coverage, then re-ran live lane selection/execution for 2026-05-28 06:38 and confirmed the loop now chooses and executes distribution_architecture_repair instead of another guard pause.
